title: LSM Video Source Inventory
key_finding: No large-scale, publicly available, ML-ready LSM video dataset exists. This is both the challenge and opportunity for the lsm-pipeline project.

sources.name: DIELSEME
sources.full_name: Diccionario de la Lengua de Señas Mexicana
sources.licensing: Government publication — requires formal permission from CONADIS
sources.notes: Uses same Cruz Aldrete framework as lsm-pipeline. Isolated signs, consistent framing.

sources.name: Spread the Sign (LSM)
sources.full_name: Spread the Sign — Lengua de Señas Mexicana
sources.licensing: Freely viewable. Prohibits downloading/redistribution without permission
sources.notes: Best standardized quality. 40+ sign languages. Consistent format enables cross-lingual research.

sources.name: Manos con Voz
sources.notes: Designed for human learners. Includes transitions/overlays requiring heavy preprocessing.

sources.name: CONADIS LSM Portal
sources.licensing: Government open-access, no explicit ML license
sources.notes: Supplementary awareness material, not structured sign data.

sources.name: YouTube LSM Channels
sources.licensing: YouTube ToS prohibits scraping. NOT suitable without creator partnership
sources.notes: Inconsistent quality. Requires individual creator partnerships.

sources.name: CINVESTAV LSM datasets
sources.licensing: Academic use — request to research group
sources.notes: Small but controlled. May include Kinect depth data. Contact research groups directly.

sources.name: UNAM IIMAS datasets
sources.notes: Internal thesis datasets. Not publicly available on standard ML repositories.

sources.name: IPN CIC datasets
sources.publisher: IPN Centro de Investigación en Computación
sources.notes: Sensor-based data could complement MediaPipe keypoint extraction.

reference_datasets_methodology.name: WLASL
reference_datasets_methodology.methodology: Curated from online dictionaries — directly applicable to LSM approach
reference_datasets_methodology.relevance: HIGH — annotation format (gloss + bbox + signer ID) is reference model

reference_datasets_methodology.name: MS-ASL
reference_datasets_methodology.methodology: URL-list distribution (metadata only, no raw video) — sidesteps copyright

reference_datasets_methodology.name: How2Sign
reference_datasets_methodology.methodology: 1080p multi-view studio capture, MediaPipe keypoints included
reference_datasets_methodology.relevance: VERY HIGH — gold standard for continuous SL, uses MediaPipe

reference_datasets_methodology.name: PHOENIX-2014-T
reference_datasets_methodology.methodology: Sourced from broadcast TV weather segments

gap_analysis.underrepresented_cm_categories: CMs #88-101 (rare configurations, proper names) likely have zero video representation
gap_analysis.underrepresented_locations: Rare body locations (e.g., 'Par' parietal, 'Je' liver) may have very few examples
gap_analysis.underrepresented_movements: Complex movements (SEVEN, ZIGZAG) are less common than STRAIGHT/ARC
gap_analysis.signer_diversity: Most sources use 1-3 signers — insufficient for signer-independent models
gap_analysis.regional_variants: Almost all sources are CDMX/national — GDL, MTY, TIJ variants underrepresented

recommendations: Priority 1: License DIELSEME and Spread the Sign LSM sections for ML use
Priority 2: Contact CINVESTAV, UNAM-IIMAS, IPN-CIC for academic dataset sharing
Priority 3: Adopt MS-ASL's URL-list distribution model for copyright compliance
Key differentiator: LSM-PN phonological annotation (101 CMs, 76 locations, movements) goes far beyond gloss-only datasets
